reasoning: Pixel art format inherently suppresses most AI tells, but adversarial inspection reveals: (1) The blade checkerboard highlight pattern is algorithmically regular — each teal/light-teal alternation is perfectly spaced in a way that suggests generation rather than hand-placement; (2) Crossguard asymmetry — at 8x zoom the left arm of the guard is 1-2px shorter/different than the right, inconsistent with deliberate pixel art craft; (3) Outline corner pixels show minor weight inconsistency (some corners have 1px diagonal, others have 2px L-turns) indicating non-uniform outline generation; (4) No AA edges detected — clean step edges throughout, positive sign; (5) No sub-pixel features detected — pixel boundaries are hard; (6) Solid areas (background, blade body) are free of noise pixels; (7) No color bleed between regions; (8) Semantic coherence is adequate — blade, guard, grip, and pommel are all present and recognizable. The sprite is functional pixel art but the overly regular highlight pattern and minor guard asymmetry are mild AI generation signatures. Not enough to fail outright, but not a clean pass.
